specific_location: Rose bush in front of house at 327 North Fork Ave.
description: BIrd was roughly the same size and shape as the ruby crowned kinglet that was feeding in the same bush. Throat and breast were yellow. Broken white eye ring (above and below the eye). Almost entire back, including cap, nape, upper tail coverts and tail, were slate gray. Mantly was olive. WIngs were gray with two distinct white wing bands. Breast and undertail coverts were white. Orange/ yellow feet contrasted with dark legsBehaviors: Bird was feeding in a large rosebush. Flitted from branch to branch and made one short fly-catching flight.Call: just a single soft buzzPlumage: winter
